body.body:
PROC_RESP_ACTION_ANNOUNCE and PROC_RESP_SELF_CORRECT are outputting their habit definition text as the actual response: "Habit executed. [PROC_RESP_SELF_CORRECT: When the employer gently corrects me...]". This appears on the web channel as Igor's reply.

ROOT CAUSE: response_template or habit dispatch is using the habit narrative/metadata as the output string instead of triggering an LLM call or canned reply.

FIX: Identify where response habits output their content. The output should either be (a) a canned response_template string, or (b) an LLM call with the habit context. Not the raw habit metadata.

INERTIA: thalamus.py = LOW. main.py = MEDIUM.

TESTS: probe that PROC_RESP_SELF_CORRECT produces a natural reply, not bracket metadata.

FORENSIC LOGGING: log_error if habit response is detected to contain its own ID string.
body.title: Habits outputting their own metadata to channel instead of response
body.result: Fixed response habits outputting metadata. Extended fallthrough check at line 3951 to reject response habits without response_template. Response habits now fall through to LLM for natural reply instead of outputting 'Habit executed. [HABIT_ID]' debug metadata. Added forensic logging (HABIT_RESPONSE_MISSING_TEMPLATE). Tests: 424 passed. Commit: a0cb5b3d. Probe deferred pending Igor restart.
